name: Bill de Blasio
subject: Video: Why I went on 'Tucker Carlson Tonight'
body:
We should never be afraid to have a dialogue with people who disagree with us.
# [](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21d2f/1630b3eb/51507b74/5f54a368/2784153919/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjcyNyJ9/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/automation-tucker-carlson?refcode=727_org_em_fr")
Friend --
Last night, I went back to Trump's favorite network to speak some truth to the millions of viewers that tune in. I've said it before and I'll say it again: I'll take on ANYONE who challenges what we're trying to do for working people.

It would be an understatement to say that Tucker Carlson and I disagree on a LOT. But we should never be afraid to have a dialogue with people who disagree with us -- especially when we have a chance to stand up for what we believe in.
**[Watch my interview on 'Tucker Carlson Tonight' now](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21d2f/1630b3eb/51507b74/5f54a36f/2784153919/VEsE/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/automation-tucker-carlson")** , and **[contribute $1 to help power this campaign:](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21d2f/1630b3eb/51507b74/5f54a368/2784153919/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjcyNyJ9/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/automation-tucker-carlson?refcode=727_org_em_fr")**
[](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21d2f/1630b3eb/51507b74/5f54a36f/2784153919/VEsC/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/automation-tucker-carlson")
As my team told you yesterday, automation is an existential threat to working people in this country. Millions of Americans will lose their jobs so that big companies can increase their profits. We can't allow automation to go unchecked as the rich get richer at the expense of working people. Even Tucker Carlson couldn't disagree with that.
**[Find out more about what I told Tucker Carlson on 'Tucker Carlson Tonight' this week](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21d2f/1630b3eb/51507b74/5f54a36f/2784153919/VEsD/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/automation-tucker-carlson"),** and **[contribute $1 to help keep me in the race.](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21d2f/1630b3eb/51507b74/5f54a368/2784153919/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjcyNyJ9/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/automation-tucker-carlson?refcode=727_org_em_fr")**
My automation plan is the ONLY plan that would provide security for workers and facilitate new, secure, well-paying jobs for the next generation of working people. It's time to protect American workers and ensure we all share in the gains of a high tech future.
Say you're with me,
Bill

name: Peter Khalil
subject: "Poor People Math"
body:
| | | | Being Poor in America is Expensive!
---
---
**—**
Ashley from Vancouver, Washington recently told me about how seemingly trivial decisions become stressful when you’re poor in America:

“Let’s do some poor people math. I live in an apartment complex. Rent is almost $1000 for 500 square feet. We all share a laundry room with 3 washers and 3 dryers. Quarters are a hot commodity.

1 roll of quarters = $10, which is 40 quarters
1 load in the wash = $1.75
1 load with the dryer = $1.75
1 complete wash and dry = $3.50 or 14 quarters
1 load of whites + 1 load of darks = $7.00

Wait, I don’t have enough for towels, sheets, blankets, delicates...I guess I can get a couple more days out of these jeans.”

Being poor in America is expensive. There are all sorts of “poverty taxes” out there that keep people from climbing the socio-economic ladder.

This campaign is determined to combat poverty. We will combat poverty by fighting for a living wage, by acknowledging that healthcare is a right, by protecting labor’s right to organize, by ensuring a vibrant job market, and by advocating for affordable housing in our cities and beyond.

We can’t fight this fight alone.

I am asking you today to donate what you can to get our message to the people. Your money gives us a megaphone. Our ads, our literature, our signage, and more, get our voice to the voters. And they cost money. Most importantly, we travel our 9,100-square-mile district every day.

Your contributions keep us going.

Recurring donations make us sustainable: As little as $5 a month, recurring, is a $60 donation by the August 2020 primary.

$10 a month is $120 by August 2020
$15 a month is $180 by August 2020
$20 a month is $240 by August 2020

To those of you who have donated – thank you! To those who haven’t, there’s still plenty of time to make a difference. I am grateful for your support.
With hope,

Peter

name: Sen. Mark Warner
subject: The truth is...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
At the end of this email, I'm going to ask you to donate to my campaign. But first I want to tell you why I'm asking.
---
| | Derek,

At the end of this email, I'm going to ask you to donate to my campaign. But first I want to tell you why I'm asking.

The truth is, I feel lucky every single day to serve the people of Virginia in the U.S. Senate, but there's still so much we need to do together, including expanding access to healthcare, securing our elections, and holding the Trump administration accountable.

But frankly, this re-election fight won't be an easy one. In 2014, I won by less than one percentage point, and back then there was no "Trump Victory Initiative" deploying Republican operatives across Virginia. The stakes are even higher this year because we know that Donald Trump sees Virginia as an important part of his path to re-election. That's why he's investing in the Commonwealth, and that's why I've been working hard to not just keep Virginia blue in 2020, but to elect Democrats up and down the ballot in 2019.

**I want to thank you for all the support you 've given me so far. But we have to work even harder right now if we're going to send Donald Trump a message and make real change happen in Virginia and across the country. Will you make your first donation to help our campaign?** Even $1 makes a difference because it all adds up on Election Day. You can give at this link: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-db](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d113/53d9897e/5287e21e/1c8e354a/592373620/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjIxMyJ9/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/warner-db?refcode=20190907nd__213__&refcode2=ask1&amount=5")**

Thank you,
Mark

name: Team Beshear
subject: sent Matt Bevin a message
body:
Thank you. We set a big goal for our financial reporting deadline yesterday and because of the strength of our grassroots team, we beat it. And we sent Matt Bevin a big message.
|
---

| | Peter,

Thank you. We set a big goal for our financial reporting deadline yesterday and because of the strength of our grassroots team, we beat it. And we sent Matt Bevin a big message.

This matters. Matt Bevin can dump millions of his own personal fortune into this race at any point. He has huge national donors behind his campaign.

We do things differently, raising most of our money from people all across Kentucky and not relying on mega-donors and personal wealth. Our campaign is about ensuring that working families have a governor fighting for them, not the well-connected special interests.

This is a team effort. We've knocked on more than a quarter-million doors so far -- that takes thousands of volunteer hours. | [](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557aa8/ad6dc73/143efba3/db47f96/3030312415/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjI0NSIsImUzdGhjMnQ5ZlE9PSI6IiJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/beshear-septreporting?refcode=20190907nd__245__&amount=")

We appreciate every dollar donated, every volunteer shift filled. subject: Resolute Reads
body:
"The number of people employed in the United States hit a record 157,878,000 in August, the 21st record set under President Donald Trump." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3d6e05088b&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [**Record 157,878,000 Employed in August**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=169e85c518&e=3ee4985eea)
-_CNS News_
“The number of people employed in the United States hit a record 157,878,000 in August, the 21st record set under President Donald Trump,” Susan Jones reports. “The unemployment rate held steady at 3.7 percent. For blacks, the unemployment rate dropped to a record low of 5.5 percent last month. And for Hispanics, the unemployment rate was 4.2 percent in August, which ties the record low set earlier this year.”

**More:** [“Job creation continues strong in August”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e0a0c697d1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [**The Washington Post’s Lost Summer**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=48ca0232be&e=3ee4985eea)
-_Washington Examiner_
“It’s been a lost summer for the _Washington Post_ ,” White House Press Secretary Stephanie Grisham and Principal Deputy Press Secretary Hogan Gidley write. “Despite record-setting accomplishments in record-setting time, data shows the news coverage of Trump has been 92% negative. The most egregious and dangerous forms of this bias thrived this summer at media outlets like the _Washington Post_ , which routinely masquerade opinion as news.”

** What the media “forgot:”** [President Trump’s summer of winning!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1caf05c5a4&e=3ee4985eea)

-_McClatchy DC_
“Ivanka Trump met with aides to Venezuelan National Assembly President Juan Guaidó in Colombia on Wednesday to underscore support for a ‘democratic transition in Venezuela’ and offer $120 million in U.S. assistance to neighboring countries under strain from a refugee crisis,” Michael Wilner reports. “We stand with the people of Venezuela in their struggle to restore democracy, freedom and rule of law,” Ms. Trump said.

-_Pittsburgh Tribune-Review_
By protecting American jobs and labor, the United States–Mexico–Canada Agreement (USMCA) is a better deal in every sense than NAFTA, the outdated pact that currently governs North American trade. “NAFTA enabled giant corporations to outsource jobs . . . Unions have railed against NAFTA for three decades. Now, their allies in Congress finally have an opportunity to scrap that deal and replace it,” Deena Flinchum writes.

-Fox News
“President Trump awarded basketball legend Jerry West the Presidential Medal of Freedom, the nation’s highest civilian honor, on Thursday, continuing a run by Trump of recognizing American sports greats . . . [The President] said West ‘richly deserved’ the medal, citing the West Virginia native's years as a decorated player, executive, and supporter of the nation’s war veterans,” Frank Miles reports.

name: Jen O'Malley Dillon
subject: A special request just for you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
In just a moment, I’m going to ask you to make your first donation to Beto’s campaign. But before I get to that, I hope you’ll give me a chance to explain why it’s so important in this immediate moment.
#
In just a moment, I’m going to ask you to make your first donation to Beto’s campaign. But before I get to that, I hope you’ll give me a chance to explain why it’s so important in this immediate moment.
**The DNC rules require a minimum number of donors to qualify for debates.** For the first debates, you needed 65,000 donors to get on stage. For the second debates, you needed 130,000. We don’t know how many will be required for the third set, but it could be 260,000 if the DNC doubles the requirement once again.
**Right now, Beto is at 213,404 donors.** If we don’t add another 47,000, we could be at risk of getting kicked off the debate stage for the third round.
**You haven’t donated to Beto yet.** Your gift could be the deciding factor in whether or not we make it to the debate. So here’s what I’m asking:
[Can you donate just $1 right now? It doesn’t have to be much — whatever you can give right now would make all the difference to us.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/32683?refcode=ema20190907-53739&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=53739%2E444211%2EQ7vz4i)
**[Give $1](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/32683?refcode=ema20190907-53739&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=53739%2E444211%2EQ7vz4i)**
Whether you’re a hardcore Beto supporter, or you’re just on our email list because you want to hear what we have to say, I hope you can agree we need Beto’s voice on that stage. If just five percent of you choose to donate today, we’ll smash the 260,000 mark and ensure we’re in a good spot to qualify.
Jen O'Malley Dillon
Campaign Manager
Beto for America

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=53739%2E444211%2EQ7vz4i). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

subject: ICYMI: POLITICO--'We either clean house, or Democrats take those seats.'
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for LaTurner
body:
'We either clean house, or Democrats take those seats.' | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bdf212baedec/icymi-politico-we-either-clean-house-or-democrats-take-those-seats?e=1050d051da)
---
| |
---
| | ‘We either clean house, or Democrats take those seats’ By [MELANIE ZANONA](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=e93747c422&e=1050d051da), POLITICO 09/07/2019 06:36 AM EDT Party officials normally hate primary challenges and all the messy drama that comes with a family feud. But this cycle, Republicans see an opportunity to _ _ clean out the dregs of the GOP. Candidates are lining up to challenge the House’s most embattled Republicans — lawmakers who have been indicted, who have made racist comments, who have faced whisper campaigns in their home states. While GOP leaders typically stay out of primary contests, these members are getting snubbed or facing outright opposition from the party establishment. At least one member of GOP leadership — retiring Rep. Paul Mitchell of Michigan — has decided to back a primary opponent to hard-line conservative Rep. Steve King (R-Iowa), who was kicked off his committees for making racist remarks earlier this year. King’s comments “reflect negatively upon Republicans and, as a result, I will contribute to his primary opponent,” Mitchell, the sophomore class representative, said in a statement. Mitchell’s stance underscores a broader feeling in the GOP conference, where many Republicans would be relieved to see fresh faces with less baggage emerge victorious in some of these primary races. Otherwise, the GOP will continue to take the reputational hit that comes with these lawmakers serving in office — or worse, the party could lose those seats in the general election. “You have a lot of people who have been concerned for many, many months now about finding some way of getting rid of some of these guys,” said Liz Mair, a GOP strategist. “There is a sense that we either clean House, or Democrats take those seats.”
## Freshman Rep. Steve Watkins of Kansas, who has recently faced rumors that he’s poised to resign amid scandal, became the latest Republican to draw a primary challenge this week. State Treasurer Jake LaTurner decided to jump into the race (and abandon his Senate bid) after receiving public encouragement from Republican former Gov. Jeff Colyer, a rare primary intervention that fueled buzz in GOP circles.
A pile of Republican candidates is also vying to take on King, who has continued to kick up controversy all year, as well as indicted Rep. Chris Collins of New York, who was arrested on insider trading charges in August 2018. And last week, former Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.) announced he was exploring whether to challenge his old colleague Rep. Duncan Hunter, who will go to trial early next year for allegedly misusing $250,000 in campaign funds to finance a lavish lifestyle. Outside groups are also itching to get involved, hoping to better position the party as Republicans try to claw their way back to power next year. The conservative Club for Growth is actively interviewing primary candidates for the Collins and Hunter races and keeping an eye on King’s district as well. “I’ve told Republican leaders: We reserve the right to be in primaries, including in challenger races,” David McIntosh, president of the Club for Growth, said in an interview. “There is a lot of tension. They don’t want us to do that.” But, he added, “We also recognize that we need to make sure the Republican majority is sustained.” King, who has been condemned by both parties for racist and inflammatory remarks, set off alarm bells in the GOP last year when he nearly lost to a Democratic challenger, despite representing a Republican stronghold in the heart of Trump country. Now, King is in the political fight of his life, as four Republican candidates — led by state Sen. Randy Feenstra — have mounted a challenge against the embattled nine-term incumbent. While the top GOP leaders don’t formally play in primaries, they certainly haven’t done King any favors. Not only did they strip him of his committee assignments for defending white supremacy and white nationalism in an interview with the New York Times, but Rep. Liz Cheney — the No. 3 Republican in the House — has called on King to resign. “As I’ve said before, it’s time for him to go. The people of Iowa’s 4th congressional district deserve better,” the Wyoming Republican tweeted last month, after King’s latest instance of eyebrow-raising rhetoric: suggesting humanity wouldn’t exist if it weren’t for rape and incest. King also [wasn’t allowed](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=ff06696e7a&e=1050d051da) to fly aboard Air Force One this summer when Trump flew to Iowa for a state GOP event — another sign of King’s pariah status in the party. The mix of snubs, demands for his resignation and lost committee assignments has left King struggling to raise money and [complaining that the party](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=4f920e601f&e=1050d051da) is trying to tip the scales against him. As a longstanding policy, the National Republican Congressional Committee does not spend in primaries. But the House GOP campaign’s arm did condemn King last cycle for a separate set of inflammatory comments and [pulled support](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=ae2196eeb4&e=1050d051da) for him shortly before Election Day. “If I were sitting there as NRCC chair, I would want to dump these guys, all three of them, in the trash,” Mair said, referring to King, Collins and Hunter. Hunter and Collins created headaches for the GOP last summer when they were both indicted, tarnishing the party’s “drain the swamp” message and sparking fears that their once-safe seats would turn competitive. Both lawmakers, however, refused to step down and narrowly defeated their Democratic challengers. Hunter has been accused by federal prosecutors of misusing campaign cash, including to pursue extramarital affairs with congressional aides and lobbyists. With Hunter at risk of facing serious jail time, Republican San Diego City Councilman Carl DeMaio has already announced a primary bid. Meanwhile, Issa — who still has friends in the House GOP after serving there nearly 20 years — is also considering jumping into the race. Rank-and-file members, like leadership, tend to stay out of ugly primary fights involving their colleagues. But Issa’s entrance into the race could complicate the equation for some of the GOP’s California delegation, who already saw their ranks dwindle after the last election. “It wouldn’t be terribly surprising to see a few — I wouldn’t say a ton — but a few members of the California delegation come out for Issa,” said Republican strategist Doug Heye. Collins, meanwhile, will go on trial in February for insider trading charges. His spokeswoman said he will “decide on re-election over the next few months,” but argued the New York Republican “remains effective in representing his constituents” and maintains “a close relationship with the White House.” Local GOP candidates see an opening and are rushing to challenge Collins, warning Republican voters that the seat could flip if Collins stays on the ticket. Like King, both Collins and Hunter have been booted from their committee assignments, handing even more ammo to their opponents who have questioned their value in Congress. “The only way this district is lost is if Chris Collins is on the ballot,” state Sen. Chris Jacobs (R-Buffalo), who declared his bid against Collins in May, [told POLITICO](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=bc5b3259a5&e=1050d051da).
## A bitter battle is also brewing in Kansas, where Watkins is facing a primary challenge from LaTurner after being dogged by [resignation rumors](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=4ed45828f6&e=1050d051da) _ _[appearing in local media](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=95197a8bec&e=1050d051da). Watkins has dismissed any suggestion that he would leave office, and his chief of staff has slammed the chatter as a "whisper campaign coming from political operatives in Kansas.”

## But concern is growing in Kansas GOP circles about Watkins’ viability as a candidate next year, according to multiple sources. Before narrowly winning his election in 2018, Watkins came under fire for [reports of sexual misconduct](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=376794f405&e=1050d051da) and for [inaccurately claiming](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=2c97b6a5ca&e=1050d051da) he started and expanded a private contracting company in the Middle East.

## LaTurner said he has not had conversations with House GOP leaders or anyone at the NRCC, but said he expected to at some point in the future.

## “At the end of the day we don’t want to see another congressional seat be turned over to the Democrats in Kansas,” LaTurner [told POLITICO](https://jakelaturner.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e36c5b1e49908a49477cee2b3&id=be9a7f1537&e=1050d051da), accusing Watkins of poor coalition building and lackluster fundraising. “Congressman Watkins, without question, puts this seat in jeopardy this cycle.”

## Watkins’ camp is already firing back, in a preview of the intraparty feud to come.
“Jake LaTurner’s entire career has been political ladder-climbing — and that climb ends in August,” said Bryan Piligra, a spokesman for Watkins. Some strategists argue that tough primary fights aren’t entirely bad for the party. The** ** winner can emerge battle-tested and better prepared to absorb attacks from Democratic opponents. But if Republicans rip each other apart in a nasty primary, it could also bruise the nominee while straining relationships inside the party. “Primaries pit families against each other. ... You will have accusations of backstabbing and being a traitor,” said Heye. “And that’s why they can become especially negative and do so very quickly.” _James Arkin and Anna Gronewold contributed to this report._
